mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL inflation trend with FEDFUNDS pinned near zero implies real rates are falling further negative, which supports equity valuations and lifts the next 20 trading-day S&P 500 return.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with CPIAUCSL rising over the prior 3 months while FEDFUNDS stays at or below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in UNRATE signals rising layoffs and weaker household income expectations, which should depress risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the signal does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of trigger occurrences or if its short-horizon hit rate is indistinguishable from 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A broad advance in RSAFS together with HOUST indicates resilient final demand and housing activity, which should support earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST expansions fail to outperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return distribution or if the joint effect disappears when either series is positive alone.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO strengthens from an already high base, it signals rising industrial activity and better cyclical earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises over 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive after two-month INDPRO acceleration, or if the effect reverses in rolling samples with no sustained positive association.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in PAYEMS combined with weaker RSAFS implies labor income is slowing while consumer spending softens, which should reduce equity cash-flow expectations and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline pattern does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the combined signal adds no predictive power beyond PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% tightens the discount rate channel and raises recession concern, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative once that threshold is breached.
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold 60-day SPX returns are not materially below pre-threshold returns, or if elevated FEDFUNDS periods do not coincide with a negative forward-return skew.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, labor-market breadth is improving and consumer demand should remain firm, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor improvement does not outperform either series individually in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips in a recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing slowdown, falling HOUST alongside weak GDPC1 signals a broad demand deceleration that should pressure corporate earnings expectations and lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint HOUST-GDPC1 weakness fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in rolling out-of-sample tests, or if the signal is positive during housing-led slowdowns.
